name: Redondela - return via Vigo Airport
name_original: Redondela - vuelta por Aeropuerto de Vigo
region_intro: Road cycling around Redondela, located in Galicia, Spain, offers diverse landscapes for cyclists. The region is characterized by its position at the mouth of the Ria de Vigo, featuring green hills, coastal views, and river valleys. Road cycling routes often traverse paved sections of the Camino de Santiago, providing a mix of terrain from mostly flat coastal paths to more challenging ascents into the surrounding hills. Best road cycling routes around Redondela The most popular road cycling route is Redondela Viaducts – Ponte Sampaio Bridge loop from Redondela-Picota, a 50.8 miles (81.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 21 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain and passes by notable architectural landmarks. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Ulló Salt Flats – Ponte Sampaio Bridge loop from Cesantes, a difficult 53.9 miles (86.8 km) path. This route offers expansive views of the Ria de Vigo and coastal scenery. Local road cyclists also love the Ponte Sampaio Bridge – Ulló Salt Flats loop from Redondela, a 39.1 miles (63.0 km) trail leading through coastal areas and river valleys, often completed in about 3 hours 7 minutes. Road cycling around Redondela is defined by coastal paths, river trails, and green hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging routes with significant elevation. The routes in Redondela are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 39 reviews. More than 700 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Redondela's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Redondela?
A: There are over 18 road cycling routes around Redondela listed on komoot, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of road cycling routes in Redondela?
A: Redondela offers a balanced mix of challenges. You'll find 9 moderate routes and 9 difficult routes, ensuring options for both intermediate and experienced cyclists. There are no easy routes specifically for road cycling listed in this guide, but the region offers varied terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Redondela?
A: Road cycling routes around Redondela feature diverse terrain, from mostly paved coastal paths along the Ria de Vigo to more challenging ascents into the surrounding green hills. Many routes traverse sections of the Camino de Santiago, which are often paved and suitable for road bikes, though some segments of the Camino might have unpaved sections. You'll encounter river valleys, coastal views, and sometimes green woodlands and vineyards.

Q: What scenic landmarks or viewpoints can I see while road cycling in Redondela?
A: Many routes offer stunning views of the Ria de Vigo, the iconic Rande Bridge, and the islands of San Simón and San Antón. You can also find lookouts such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/921665" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de Paralaia&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4794959" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Vigo estuary&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also known for its 'Best Bench in the World' viewpoint, offering spectacular vistas over the Ria de Vigo.

Q: Are there any historical sites or cultural points of interest along the routes?
A: Yes, Redondela is a convergence point for the Camino de Santiago, so you'll encounter historical elements. Routes often pass by the historic Island of San Simón in the Ria de Vigo, and the Roman bridge of Pontesampaio over the Verdugo River. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1271271" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Redondela Viaducts – Ponte Sampaio Bridge loop from Redondela-Picota&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights notable architectural landmarks.

Q: Are there long-distance road cycling options in Redondela?
A: Redondela is a key hub for the Camino Portugués, offering opportunities for longer rides. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17552090" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ulló Salt Flats – Ponte Sampaio Bridge loop from Cesantes&lt;/a&gt; cover over 53 miles (86 km), and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1271271" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Redondela Viaducts – Ponte Sampaio Bridge loop from Redondela-Picota&lt;/a&gt; is over 50 miles (81 km), providing significant distances for road cyclists.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes around Redondela?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in Redondela are designed as loops. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38222606" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponte Sampaio Bridge – Ulló Salt Flats loop from Redondela&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through coastal areas and river valleys, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17553352" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponte do Burgo – Ponte Sampaio Bridge loop from Redondela&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Redondela?
A: The region generally offers pleasant conditions for cycling. While the Río Tomeza Riverside Trail can be prone to flooding in the rainy season, many paved routes are enjoyable year-round. Spring and autumn typically provide mild temperatures and beautiful scenery, making them ideal for exploring the diverse landscapes.

Q: Is parking available near road cycling trailheads in Redondela?
A: Yes, Redondela, being a significant hub, offers various parking options within the town and near popular starting points for routes. Specific parking details may vary by route, but general town parking is available.

Q: How accessible are Redondela's road cycling routes by public transport?
A: Redondela is well-connected by public transport, particularly as a key point on the Camino de Santiago. This makes it relatively easy to access starting points for many cycling routes via local buses or trains, especially if you're arriving from larger nearby cities like Vigo or Pontevedra.

Q: Where can I find refreshments or places to stop along the routes?
A: Given Redondela's role as a Camino de Santiago stop, you'll find numerous cafes, restaurants, and small shops in the town itself and in the villages along the more popular routes. These provide excellent opportunities for refreshments and breaks during your ride.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Redondela?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 39 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, combining coastal views with green hills and river valleys, and the well-maintained paved sections that make for enjoyable road cycling.
tips: Redondela is today better known as "The town of viaducts" for the two recognizable railway viaducts that give it its own personality: the "Madrid Viaduct", from 1876, and the "Pontevedra Viaduct", from 1884. Both were declared Monuments Historic-Artistic

Climb of about 5 kms from Redondela to Vigo / Peinador Airport, average slope of approximately 5%, although with sections with more than 10% and some constant km at 8 or 9%, nice views going up towards the Galleiro mountains, and if you are lucky to get to the end you can still see a plane take off or land at practically the same height.
5km stretch of road from the airport to National Highway 550. Mainly downhill with a climb in the middle

waypoints.name: Vilar de Infesta Climb to Peinador Airport and Galleiro Mountains View
